target: Per-equation Vec<Value> allocation in the CPU executor's single-output path

lever.description: Add fj-interpreters::eval_equation_single: for a sub-jaxpr-free, single-output equation it resolves inputs and calls eval_primitive directly, returning the lone Value. Previously the executor's evaluate_equation called eval_equation_outputs -> eval_primitive_multi, which for every non-multi-output primitive returns vec![eval_primitive(..)] (a one-element heap Vec) that evaluate_equation immediately pops. Multi-output primitives (Qr/Lu/Svd/Eigh/TopK/Slogdet/Eig) and sub-jaxpr (cond/scan/while/switch) equations delegate to the unchanged multi path.

benchmarks.method: rch Criterion, same worker back-to-back (baseline = evaluate_equation reverted to multi+pop, optimized = eval_equation_single)
benchmarks.honesty_note: An initial cross-worker comparison spuriously suggested ~2.2x; the figures above are the honest same-worker back-to-back result (~3-12%). Modest but real, broad (every jaxpr equation), and zero-risk.

score.note: Opportunity-matrix score (Impact x Confidence / Effort), not a speedup multiple. Speedup is ~3-12%.

isomorphism_proof.structural: For single-output, sub-jaxpr-free equations eval_primitive_multi(p, inputs) == vec![eval_primitive(p, inputs)] (see fj-lax::eval_primitive_multi `_` arm). eval_equation_single returns eval_primitive(p, inputs) directly; the previous path returned vec![..] then popped it. Identical Value, identical errors (resolve_equation_inputs + eval_primitive unchanged).
isomorphism_proof.multi_output: Qr/Lu/Svd/Eigh/TopK/Slogdet/Eig and sub-jaxpr equations delegate to eval_equation_outputs, byte-for-byte the prior behavior.
isomorphism_proof.ordering: Execution/scheduling order unchanged; only the per-equation result wrapping is elided.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.behavioral_proof: No synthetic golden-bits file: this is an allocation-elision refactor, not a new numeric kernel, so output values are structurally unchanged and verified by the full test suites — 60 fj-interpreters + 130 fj-backend-cpu unit tests (which execute jaxprs through CpuBackend and assert outputs) all pass.
